definition: Encode images, patches, or tiles with a domain-appropriate supervised, self-supervised, or foundation image encoder and compute train-eval embedding similarity. This audits visual/perceptual support overlap, especially when pixel or histogram distances do not capture the semantic image shift.
example_usage: python embedding_cosine_similarity.py --train train_image_embeddings.csv --eval eval_image_embeddings.csv --out pairwise_similarity.csv --embedding-cols-prefix emb_

citations.title: A Domain-specific Perceptual Metric via Contrastive Self-supervised Representation: Applications on Natural and Medical Images

evidence.summary: The paper motivates representation-based perceptual similarity and domain-specific self-supervised image encoders.
It computes perceptual similarity using feature maps from pretrained networks.
Domain-specific representations better capture medical image similarity and can extend to 3D vision.

limitations: Embedding similarity inherits biases and domain coverage of the encoder.
Perceptual similarity can disagree with clinically or scientifically relevant similarity.
Patch-level and scan-level aggregation choices can change the axis.
quality_gates: Report encoder, pretraining data, layer/pooling choice, patch extraction, and aggregation method.
Compare against scanner/protocol metadata and histogram/radiomics similarity in medical imaging.
Inspect nearest neighbors to detect shortcut features such as borders, compression, or acquisition artifacts.
